title: silene menziesii var. viscosa
intro: Silene menziesii var. viscosa, also known as Sticky Catchfly or Sticky Mountain Catchfly, is a variety of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Silene menziesii var. viscosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a useful herbal remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin or used as a poultice to treat various health conditions.
